evals.prompt: I am building a support ticket classifier but I have zero training data. I need to generate synthetic examples for 5 categories: billing, bug, how-to, feature-request, and account.
evals.expected_output: A generator signature whose outputs match the task inputs, 5-10 hand-written seed examples with .with_inputs(), category-driven batch generation, quality filtering, and a train/dev split for optimization.

assertions.name: generator_mirrors_task
assertions.description: Generator signature outputs match the task signature inputs (e.g., generator outputs ticket_text which is the task input)

assertions.name: seed_examples_with_inputs
assertions.description: Seed examples use .with_inputs() to mark input fields

assertions.name: generates_per_category
assertions.description: Generates examples for each category to ensure coverage

assertions.name: includes_filtering
assertions.description: Filters generated examples for quality using metric-based or LM-based assessment

assertions.name: provider_agnostic
assertions.description: LM config uses generic provider with alternative comment

evals.prompt: My email classifier works at 85% but fails on sarcastic emails and emails with mixed languages. I have 200 real examples but these edge cases are underrepresented. How do I generate more data for these specific gaps?
evals.expected_output: Scenario-driven generation targeting the specific failure patterns (sarcasm, mixed language), with edge case scenarios as inputs to the generator, plus quality filtering.

assertions.name: scenario_driven_generation
assertions.description: Uses scenario-driven generation targeting specific edge cases (sarcasm, mixed language) rather than generic category-driven

assertions.name: edge_case_scenarios
assertions.description: Defines specific scenarios matching the failure patterns the user described

assertions.name: augments_existing_data
assertions.description: Adds generated data to the existing 200 real examples rather than replacing them

assertions.name: quality_filtering
assertions.description: Filters generated examples before mixing with real data

evals.prompt: I need to generate training data for an invoice extraction task. The AI should pull out vendor name, date, total amount, and line items from invoice text. I cannot use real customer invoices for privacy reasons.
evals.expected_output: A multi-field generator that takes known field values as inputs and generates realistic invoice text containing them, with privacy-safe synthetic values and assessment-based filtering.

assertions.name: multi_field_generator
assertions.description: Generator takes all expected output fields (name, date, amount, items) as inputs and generates the invoice text as output

assertions.name: synthetic_pii
assertions.description: Uses synthetic vendor names, dates, and amounts rather than real customer data

assertions.name: with_inputs_marks_task_inputs
assertions.description: Generated Examples mark the invoice text as input and extracted fields as expected outputs

assertions.name: assessment_filtering
assertions.description: Uses LM-based assessment to verify generated invoices are realistic and fields are correctly embedded
